title: Start the Uniface IDE
text: Start the Uniface IDE You can start the Uniface IDE from the default shortcut, or you can define your own shortcut with different command line parameters. By default, the Uniface IDE is automatically installed and configured to use SQLite as the Repository. If you configure Uniface to use another database, the database must be running before starting the IDE. Start the Uniface IDE from the default shortcut created during installation. By default, the command line dialog is displayed, so that you can instruct the IDE to perform a specific action or use a different configuration. Uniface remembers previously used command line parameters, so you can also choose a command line from the drop-drown list. Note: This dialog is displayed because the shortcut for the Uniface IDE specifies a question mark parameter ( ? ) in its command line. You can edit this shortcut or create your own with different command line switches. Click OK to continue. At startup, Uniface checks whether the default templates are available in the Repository. If they are not found, you are prompted to load them. The defaults mustbe loaded in your Repository. If you want to import an alternative set of templates, click the Browse button to find and import them. Related Links Uniface Command Line Interface Uniface provides a command line interface that enables you to access many functions without actually opening the user interface. By running a Uniface executable (such as ide.exe or uniface) with command line switches you can compile objects, debug applications, import and export, specify assignment files, and more. Uniface Default Templates Uniface provides a default set of templates and snippets that enable you to create new development objects and components that can work immediately. In fact, it is not possible to create a new object without a template. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
